title: 'Hamnet' Wins Best Picture, Drama at 2026 Golden Globes
text: Director Chloé Zhao's historical drama Hamnet claimed the top prize for dramatic film at the 2026 Golden Globes ceremony on Sunday. Producer Steven Spielberg accepted the award alongside Zhao. Spielberg praised Zhao's vision, stating: I felt there was really only one filmmaker on the face of the planet who could tell this story. In her acceptance speech, Zhao acknowledged the film's cast and crew, noting that some members lost their loved ones while we were making this film. She reflected on a conversation with co-star Paul Mescal about vulnerability and artistic authenticity, emphasizing: the most important thing of being an artist is learning to be vulnerable enough to allow ourselves to be seen. The competing drama nominees included Netflix's Frankenstein adaptation by Guillermo del Toro, plus Neon-backed films It Was Just an Accident, The Secret Agent, and Sentimental Value. Warner Bros.' Sinners, a period piece blending prestige drama with vampire horror, rounded out the field. The film earned six total Golden Globe nominations, including Best Screenplay and Best Original Score. Co-star Jessie Buckley won Best Female Performance in a Drama, while Paul Mescal received a nomination for Best Male Supporting Performance.
